items.title: USDA approves Arkansas SNAP waiver restricting soda, candy, and some sweetened drinks starting July 1, 2026
items.summary: USDA approved Arkansas’ request to exclude soda (including low/no-calorie), candy, and fruit/vegetable drinks with less than 50% juice from SNAP purchases, effective July 1, 2026, for two years. The state’s waiver modification was approved May 12, 2026, and the waiver is listed with a 07/01/26 target implementation date.

items.title: USDA lists dozens of state SNAP food-restriction waivers, with multiple new restrictions rolling out in 2026
items.summary: USDA’s SNAP waiver tracker shows many states implementing restrictions on items like soda and candy in 2026, including Florida (04/20/26), Texas (04/01/26), and Nebraska (01/01/26, with candy added effective Nov. 1, 2026). The tracker also lists upcoming target dates such as Colorado (10/30/26) and Virginia (10/01/26), signaling continued state policy changes.

items.title: CMS Medicaid work-requirements rule sets 2027 start and tightens medical frailty exemption, KFF analysis says
items.summary: KFF reports CMS issued an interim final rule on June 1, 2026 requiring 43 states to condition Medicaid eligibility for ACA expansion adults on meeting community engagement (work) requirements starting Jan. 1, 2027 (or sooner). The rule limits medical-frailty exemptions by tying them to ability to comply and prohibiting states from adding exemption categories; it also allows self-declaration through 2027, with broader documentation requirements beginning in January 2028.

items.title: Education Department finalizes ‘Workforce Pell’ for short-term programs starting July 1, 2026
items.summary: The U.S. Department of Education says its final rule allows students to receive Pell Grants for eligible short-term workforce programs beginning July 1, 2026. ED says programs can prepare people for immediate employment in as little as 8 weeks and must meet time/length requirements and outcome metrics.

items.title: Federal Student Aid launches real-time FAFSA results (APP-26-06)
items.summary: Federal Student Aid announced the launch of “Real-Time FAFSA Results” in an electronic announcement posted June 1, 2026 (APP-26-06). The update is intended to speed up FAFSA processing feedback for applicants and schools, according to FSA’s announcement.

items.title: HUD opens FY 2026 Continuum of Care homelessness NOFO totaling $4.04B, including $1.3B for new projects
items.summary: HUD announced a Fiscal Year 2026 Continuum of Care (CoC) Notice of Funding Opportunity totaling $4.04 billion to fund homelessness assistance nationwide. HUD said the NOFO includes $1.3 billion for new projects, prioritizing Transitional Housing and Supportive Service projects alongside housing assistance and wraparound services.

items.title: IRS: 27 states opt into new Federal Scholarship Tax Credit program, offering up to a $1,700 credit
items.summary: The IRS said 27 states elected to participate in the Federal Scholarship Tax Credit (FSTC) program (IR-2026-76, June 8, 2026). The program lets eligible taxpayers claim a federal tax credit of up to $1,700 for qualified contributions to Scholarship Granting Organizations that fund K-12 education scholarships.

items.title: IRS updates employer child care credit for tax year 2026: up to $500k ($600k for small businesses)
items.summary: The IRS says for amounts paid after Dec. 31, 2025, the employer-provided child care credit equals 40% of qualified child care expenditures (50% for eligible small businesses), plus 10% of qualified resource and referral expenditures. The credit is capped at $500,000 per year ($600,000 for eligible small businesses), and the IRS notes the small-business gross receipts test is generally $32,000,000 for tax years beginning in 2026.
